groups.name: Tools You Find in a Workshop

easy.notes: Four simple, concrete groups: school items, kitchen items, farm animals, and handy tools. Each group uses everyday objects kids recognize visually or by use.

medium.notes: Each group pairs second parts with a common prefix (rain-, sun-, snow-, fire-) to form familiar compound words (rainbow, rainforest; sunflower, sunrise; snowball, snowman; fire alarm, fire station). Cross-over temptation note: some items could look like they belong in another prefix group, but the intended grouping uses the second-part word with its specific prefix.

groups.name: Words That Read the Same Backward
Common Musical Terms that Are Everyday Words

hard.notes: Hard puzzles lean into abstract connections and multi-meaning words. Group 1 highlights everyday words that act as both noun and verb; Group 2 uses color-name fruits; Group 3 features true palindromes; Group 4 uses musical terms that are also common words. Cross-over temptation exists: several items could plausibly fit more than one group (for example, 'note' can be a musical term or a thing you jot down), but each item has a single best fit in its assigned group.
